manager_bullets: Kevin ran an effective peer-to-peer discovery call by leading with questions, sharing credible examples from his own work, and positioning himself as seeking feedback rather than pitching. Jacob became a genuine thought partner rather than a prospect.

The conversation surfaced a real, specific pain point (manual daily briefing process for sales leaders) that could become a viable wedge for Kevin's business, and Jacob validated the problem from multiple angles.

Kevin missed an opportunity to establish a concrete next step with Jacob—no explicit follow-up, no agreement for Jacob to intro him to others, no timeline for a follow-up call. This should have been closed more tightly given the strong rapport.

listening_ratio.quote: What would you change? Update what's actually valuable, what's not valuable? Because I have my perspective from like a rep side of things.
listening_ratio.feedback: Kevin consistently asked Jacob open-ended questions and gave him space to share his perspective as a buyer. He explicitly positioned himself as seeking feedback rather than pitching, creating a true peer dialogue throughout the call.

problem_surfacing.quote: I have like a really manual process around this...every day a sales manager has calls coming up...having it send me a daily brief in my inbox that I can just read while I have my coffee in the morning, that is the biggest unlock I think for me.
problem_surfacing.feedback: Jacob articulated a sharp, specific pain point about his daily workflow and the manual process of consolidating information. Kevin then dug deeper into whether email or dashboard delivery would be better, showing genuine curiosity about the exact shape of the problem.

credibility_moments.quote: So I gave this over to marketing and now marketing is doing blast outreach using this whole infrastructure to all the accounts that are not owned by AES...your entire account list of ICP targets is hit once a month.
credibility_moments.feedback: Kevin shared multiple concrete examples of systems he's built and deployed at his current company, including specific structures (top 50 accounts, 4,000 ICP accounts), templates, and results. This made him feel like a peer who had already solved the problem at scale.

next_step_clarity.quote: I'm chatting with center next week too. Ask him. I reached out to Rick, so we're gonna chat in a few weeks.
next_step_clarity.feedback: Kevin mentioned upcoming conversations with other people but didn't establish a concrete next step with Jacob himself. While the call ended warmly with mutual enthusiasm, there was no explicit agreement about when they'd reconnect or what Jacob would do to help Kevin validate his ideas.

curiosity_unlocked.quote: I love what you built man. I think the services piece is super interesting. But if you have a templated software you can sell as well, I think it's a home run...you just gotta find your industry niche and then bro, like I think you just chase it down.
curiosity_unlocked.feedback: Jacob became highly engaged and offered unsolicited advice, specific industry ideas (financial advisors, regulated industries), and concrete encouragement. He even shared that the conversation was inspiring him to think about his own business during his upcoming sabbatical, showing deep curiosity and investment in Kevin's success.

listening_ratio.quote: So I'd love to kind of get your perspective on, like, what you did to go from where I am to where you are, which is like, you've got leads, you're, you're running a successful business.
listening_ratio.feedback: Kevin opened with a genuine question and spent most of the call drawing Nathan out about his journey, systems, and approach. He listened actively, asked follow-up questions about specifics (APIs, Claude, terminal setup), and let Nathan fully explain his business model rather than redirecting to his own product.

problem_surfacing.quote: I'm wondering if I'll run into friction there. Whereas, like, if I were to just be like, hey, I'll go to dental offices and like, just go in and like, you know, hey, I can run an automated, you know, people call after hours and things like that.
problem_surfacing.feedback: Kevin surfaced real strategic uncertainties about target market selection and whether to focus on AI-first or traditional businesses. However, these remained somewhat exploratory rather than landing on a sharp, specific problem to solve. Nathan's advice about 'just talk to your customers' prevented deeper problem diagnosis.

credibility_moments.quote: That's what I've done with like co work. When I started like a month and a half ago, I was like, I have no idea how any of this stuff works, but it's just like, oh cool, you kind of figure it out as you go.
credibility_moments.feedback: Kevin shared concrete examples of his own building journey and mistakes, positioning himself as a peer learning in the same space. He also mentioned specific tools (cowork, Claude) and demonstrated hands-on experience, making him relatable and credible despite being earlier in his journey than Nathan.

next_step_clarity.quote: Okay. I feel like this helped me sort of like, cement a little bit of more of, like, what I'm gonna talk about on these calls, too. Like, hey, I've got this thing. But like you're the buyer, what do you want?
next_step_clarity.feedback: Kevin articulated a concrete takeaway—shifting his sales calls to focus on buyer needs rather than pushing his product—and has scheduled follow-up calls with old VPs. However, there's no explicit commitment to specific actions or timeline beyond 'I'm gonna talk about this on these calls.'

curiosity_unlocked.quote: Are you building like N8N sort of like connectors and stuff like that with them or like, how do you fulfill on those things? Let me, I want to show you one more thing.
curiosity_unlocked.feedback: Nathan asked multiple technical follow-up questions (API vs MCP, token costs, terminal setup) and became increasingly engaged as Kevin showed genuine curiosity. Nathan voluntarily offered to demo his system and share additional insights, showing strong buy-in to helping Kevin succeed.
